entry.title: SAP GTS - Legal Changes Hot info - April Edition
entry.content:
NL DMS:  Digitaal Dossier for document submission in the Netherlands

The Regulatory change manager (RCM) has been updated with information on the Digital Dossier legal change HERE

The planned delivery date is 31 Aug. 2026, as outlined in the announcement: 

  • The Digitaal Dossier is a new Dutch Customs initiative that modernizes how companies submit documents for import and export declarations.
  • The legal change is in a transition phase. The new standard procedure is expected to be established by May 16, 2026. A potential transitional period is being further developed. Customs by default expects companies to arrange the necessary connection at the starting date of the Digitaal Dossier functionality by May 16th. As the delivery of this legal change is not planned at this point but during the transitional phase, please inform Dutch Customs no later than May 1st by sending an E-Mail to Douane.Digitaal.Dossier@douane.nl.
    Consequently the published due date is an estimate until final timeline is communicated by customs authorities.

Given this information, if relevant for your business, please contact asap the Douane email as above,  to state that the the May 16th date cannot be met by your company, as not informing Customs may lead to further issues. 

 

PASSAR NE015 v5:  With increment DFEA-4210, Swiss customs introduced version 5 of the export declaration (NE015). The new message version includes enhancements for warehouse traffic, partial deliveries, and other changes. A migration to version 5 is absolutely necessary to continue to enable the message exchange.

A number of notes are currently available around the changes.
Please refer to note:
3720167 - CH Passar export: Changeover and adjustments for version 5 of export declaration (NE015 v5)

Additional Notes: 

3740133CH Passar Export: Incompleteness check for version 5 of export declaration (NE015 v5) - part 2
3624365FAQ and corrections for Passar 1.0 export
3720168CH Passar Export: Incompleteness check for version 5 of export declaration (NE015 v5)
3713392CH Passar Export: XML Definition and XML Mapping for Version 5 of Export Declaration (NE015 v5)

Please note:Notes related to the change of decimal places for weights and quantities are in process and will be released in due course. (as of 27.04.26)
3744948 CH Passar Ausfuhr: XML-Mapping für Gewichte und Mengen mit 3-Nachkommastellen (NE015 v5)
3744949  CH Passar Ausfuhr: Datenbeschaffung für Gewichte und Mengen mit 3 Nachkommastellen (NE015 v5) 
3744950  CH Passar Ausfuhr: Unvollständigkeitsprüfung für Gewichte und Mengen mit 3-Nachkommastellen (NE015 v5)

entry.title: Re: Advanced FCV unrealized posting remains open after pa...
entry.content:
Hello!

 

Key principle behind Advanced Foreign Currency Valuation

  • Unrealized FX differences are created, updated, and reversed only by the valuation run
  • Operational postings (invoice clearing, payment postings) do not touch valuation postings
  • Valuation postings are temporary accounting adjustments valid as of a specific key date, not permanent changes to the transactional document chain

 

What exactly happens in presented scenario (step by step):

Step 1 – Supplier invoice

  • Invoice posted: 1,000 USD
  • Vendor open item carries:
  • • Transaction currency: USD
  • • Local currency translated at invoice exchange rate

No valuation impact yet.

 

Step 2 – Period-end Foreign Currency Valuation run

  • Valuation key date: e.g. 31.03
  • Exchange rate difference as loss
  • System posts:
  • • 100 EUR unrealized FX loss
  • • Counter-account: FX valuation adjustment account
  • Vendor item is valuation-adjusted, but:
  • • Original invoice document is untouched
  • • Valuation is stored as a separate valuation layer

 

Step 3 – Payment is posted (before next valuation)

When the payment is posted:

  • The system calculates realized FX difference vs. the original invoice amount
  • It ignores valuation adjustments
  • Result:
  • • A realized FX difference at clearing
  • • The valuation posting (100 EUR) remains open and posted

This is because:

Foreign Currency Valuation adjustments are not part of the clearing logic, are not transactional corrections

 

Step 4 – Next valuation run

  • System detects:
  • • Original open item no longer exists
  • Therefore:
  • • The previous unrealized valuation (100 EUR) is automatically reversed
  • Net effect over time:
  • • Unrealized FX → reversed
  • • Realized FX remains

Accounting result is correct over the full lifecycle

 

SAP does not reverse valuation at payment posting because:

  1. Valuation belongs to a key date

Unrealized FX differences are only valid as of a reporting date (e.g. period end).

If SAP reversed valuation at clearing time:

  • It would mix operational events with reporting adjustments
  • This would break auditability and valuation traceability

 

  1. SAP separates:
  • Operational accounting (invoices, payments, clearing)
  • Reporting adjustments (valuation, impairment, accruals)

Only the valuation engine is allowed to:

  • Create
  • Update
  • Reverse valuation postings

 

  1. Ledger and parallel accounting consistency

In S/4HANA Cloud:  Foreign Currency Valuation often runs per ledger and invoices are not Ledger specific

 

From an accounting point of view:

  •  Financial statements at each closing date are correct
  •  Intra-period balances may look counter-intuitive between:
  • Payment date and
  • Next valuation run

you temporarily see:

  • A valuation posting without an open foreign currency vendor item because:
  • Unrealized FX is assessed only at reporting dates
  • No requirement exists to clear valuation immediately when clearing occurs

 

There is also the option to run Foreign Currency Valuation more often than once per month if is needed.

entry.title: Re: Week 4: AI Developer Challenge SAP-RPT-1 Model
entry.content:
User ID: GE311613

Thank you for the challenge! To get the correct predictions, I modified the following fields in the CSV:

  1. Changed the SALESGROUP for "Desktop Computer" and "Macbook" from "Enterprise Solutions" to "Consumer Electronics".

After these changes, the model correctly predicted:

  • Laptop -> Consumer Electronics

  • Office Chair -> Office Furniture

entry.title: Re: Week 3 - Use AI Agents with Grounding Tools
entry.content: Week 3 completed! UserId:  GE306937

entry.title: Re: Live Session: Get Certified - SAP Generative AI Developer | New Dates Added – Register Now!
entry.content: Unlock your future in AI with our exclusive May live session series: Get Certified: SAP Certified - SAP Generative AI Developer! Join Sidharth Gupta on May 6th, 13th, and 20th for interactive sessions covering essential topics, practical demonstrations, and exam strategies. Whether you’re a beginner or experienced developer, these sessions will guide you through SAP’s Generative AI tools, hands-on exercises, and certification prep. Don’t miss this opportunity to boost your skills and get ready for the C_AIG certification. Secure your spot today and take the next step in your SAP career! Register now for the May sessions!

entry.title: Re: 3XK In-House Repair – Spare Parts Consumption Timing...
entry.content: Hi Chris, Thanks a lot for the detailed explanation — really helpful and confirms our understanding of the 3XK behaviour. We’ll do some further testing with competing demands (e.g. sales orders) to see how reservation and ATP behave in practice. Based on that, we’ll assess whether the standard process is sufficient or if we need to consider a small extension for better control between warehouse and service. Appreciate your help! Best regards, Wei

entry.title: Unlock the Power of Artificial Intelligence with SAP Learning: Courses, Certifications & More
entry.content:
Artificial Intelligence (AI) is transforming the way businesses operate, making it crucial to stay up-to-date with the latest advancements and best practices. SAP Learning is at the forefront of this revolution, offering a comprehensive suite of training opportunities tailored to boost your expertise and career in AI.

Featured Learning on SAP: Joule

Meet Joule—SAP’s intelligent AI assistant designed to accelerate business transformation. Whether you’re new to Joule or aiming to integrate AI-driven agents throughout your organizational system landscape, SAP’s curated courses will help you harness its full potential:

  • Introducing Joule: Get started with the basics and discover how Joule is reshaping intelligent business processes.
  • Boosting AI-driven Business Transformation with Joule Agents: Learn to deploy AI agents that unlock new efficiencies and innovation.
  • Implementing Joule Across Your Organizational System Landscape: Dive into a guided journey for seamless integration across your enterprise.

AI Foundation: Build, Solve, and Certify

Ready to put generative AI to work? SAP offers hands-on training and globally recognized certifications:

  • Solve Your Business Problems Using Prompts and LLMs in SAP Generative AI Hub: Leverage large language models (LLMs) and intelligent prompts to address real-world challenges.
  • SAP Certified – SAP Generative AI Developer: Validate your expertise and showcase your skill set with this in-demand certification, tailored for today’s data-driven environment.

Explore More: Expert-Led Sessions & Learning Journeys

SAP Learning goes beyond traditional courses. Benefit from expert-led live sessions and immersive, guided learning paths that ensure you not only gain knowledge but also practical, real-world skills. Our Artificial Intelligence Featured Learning Page is your one-stop shop to discover the full spectrum of AI offerings.

Why Choose SAP Learning for AI?

  • Skill-building for the future: Acquire knowledge that aligns with the latest business needs and technological breakthroughs.
  • Expert guidance: Learn from top professionals and AI practitioners.
  • Certification: Stand out with credentials that demonstrate your AI expertise to employers and peers.

Take the next step in your AI journey with SAP Learning.
Master artificial intelligence, get certified, and shape the future of business innovation. Visit the Artificial Intelligence Featured Learning Page to get started today!

entry.title: Re: Hire Template Configuration: Supported MDF Object Types and Implementation Guidelines for Onboar
entry.content:
I don’t understand the reasoning behind this limitation.
One of the key selling points of Onboarding 2.0 at launch was that it would remain on the same framework as Employee Central, so this seems inconsistent with that expectation.

entry.title: Re: Mass Sales Order Creation via Excel Upload – Handling...
entry.content: faced the same challenge building a similar solution. The key insight: separate the data preparation layer (Excel → mapped fields) from the SAP upload step. Happy to share what worked for field mapping and required vs optional fields if useful.

Descubre cómo integrar la gestión del cambio, involucrar a los equipos y medir resultados para fortalecer la resiliencia organizacional. ¡Una sesión ideal para líderes y profesionales enfocados en el éxito del cambio continuo!

¡No te lo pierdas!

entry.title: How to implement Data Privacy and Retention Architecture in Success Factors
entry.content:
Hi Community 

I would like to understand  how data retention rules are configured in your organisations. 

What configuration options are available within SuccessFactors to support retention and deletion
Any constraints or risks we should be aware of
Recommended approach and next steps to move this into delivery

Thanks

entry.title: Re: How has RISE with SAP changed ownership across teams...
entry.content:
Customers that are already under SAP RISE, I'd say it's an operational model different for Basis people, as per SAP RISE roles and responsibilities

SAP is in charge of managing the lower level of an SAP System, such as infra, OS, Networking, OS patching, etc., but also at some higher level, such as access to the 000, which is limited to SAP and activities that only SAP can do.

From an operational perspective, the customer will require a partner or internal staff to more strategically and be the bridge to translate Business Requirements to Technical ones when it is required, such as:

  • Coordinating maintenance windows
  • Opening requests to the SAP RISE team to request something that only SAP can do
  • Proper planning for the request between the organization and SAP RISE support to achieve the desired outcome.

I would say it is not simple because of this operational model itself, but it is more strategic and different.

Shifting from tradditional operational model to this SAP RISE is something to embrace and pushes us to have a new mindset.

entry.title: Validation Error: Exception Subprocess should start with Error Start Event and end with End Message
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am currently working on an Integration Flow,I’m running into a persistent validation error that is preventing a clean deployment.

The Error Message:

"Exception Subprocess should start with an Error Start Event and end with either End Message or Error End".

 

 

Any insights or best practices on how to resolve these validation flags would be greatly appreciated!

Best regards,

entry.title: BTP User Cannot Receive “Forgot Password” Email After Being Added in Subaccount
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I am encountering an issue related to user creation and password reset in SAP BTP.

Scenario:
- I created a user in a BTP subaccount via Security → Users.
- The user was successfully created and assigned roles.

Issue:
- When the user tries to log in and clicks "Forgot Password", no password reset email is received.
- The email address is correct and has been verified.
- The Spam/Junk folder has also been checked.

Important observation:
- My own email address was not created in the IAS tenant, but I was still able to receive emails.

Question:
- What configuration is required to enable password reset emails to be sent successfully?

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: cross-company STO VL02N Purchase order XXXXXX does not contain items for a stock transfer
entry.content:
In a cross-company STO scenario:

1. I created an NB-type stock transfer purchase order via ME21N, maintaining all required data including shipping details, supplying plant, and receiving plant.

2. I then created an NLCC-type delivery document via VL10B, which completed without any errors.

3. When attempting to post goods issue (PGI) via VL02N using movement type 643 (two-step cross-company transfer), the system throws error M7153: "Purchase order XXXXXX does not contain items for a stock transfer."

Could you please help me resolve this issue?  Thank you very much.

entry.title: Re: SF Learning: Online Session timeout
entry.content:
Hello Ramya,

In the application we already have a setting that was introduced to help customers who had issues when their users were taking long online courses. If a user takes a long online course, the BizX session could time out and prevent the completion from being communicated to the Learning.

This Learning setting (sessionMgmtNotifyModuleActivity) prevents the BizX session from timing out while a user is running an online course in Learning.

Please Follow the below steps:

  1. Please navigate to System Administration > Configuration > System Configuration > BizX >
  2. Search for the property sessionMgmtNotifyModuleActivity=false and check that if it is set to false. If the property is set to false, then only the browser based ping to BizX is used 
  3. Set it to true to ensure that Session Management service ping is used for keep alive of BizX session. With this setting, Learning is pinging BizX every 5 minutes in order to keep the session alive.

Important Checks On Customer Side: if you are still having issues navigating through Learning Management System(LMS) and content after checking the above setting, it may be getting blocked your side. Ensure your internal IT checks that your Internal Proxies are not blocking any connections between Learning Management System(LMS)and BizX.

With regards to adding a warning, you can probably look at the below idea that was already submitted & is currently under review status.

https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/263029

entry.title: Wrong Data on Invoice
entry.content:
Hey guys, 

I require a change for the invoice address on the invoice with number CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER. Invoice is already paid. 

I got the invoice from me.sap.com --> Finance & Legal --> Billing --> Open Invoices

I cannot get invoice specific assistance as the button for it is greyed out. 

Please mail me the correct invoice. 
Thanks in advance!


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Wrong Data on Invoice
entry.content:
Dear customer,

If you need to change the invoice address on the invoice, and you cannot click 'Get Assistance' button in the Invoices card because the button is grey-out,

then could you pls kindly refer to KBA 3107749 and contact the SAP Cash Collection team by sending email to FinanceAR@sap.com ?

In the email, provide your invoice/billing questions, your customer number/name and full details and screenshots of the invoice/invoice number. 

Thanks and best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: HANA Cockpit retention not deleting old SYSTEMDB backup generations although log_mode is normal
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am testing HANA Cockpit backup retention for SYSTEMDB@HSD and would like to clarify if the behavior I am seeing is expected.

Configuration:

  • Backup destination: File
  • Backup path: /hana/shared/HSD/HDB00/backup/data/SYSTEMDB/
  • Daily complete data backup: around 02:00 AM
  • Automatically Delete Backup Generations: Yes
  • Deletion option: Remove from backup catalog and delete physically from file system
  • Retain Backup Generations Younger Than: 1 day
  • Minimum No. of Retained Backup Generations: 1
  • Start Daily Automatic Deletion: 9:00 PM UTC
  • Assigned Database User: SYSTEM
  • log_mode = normal

On the morning of Apr 28, I still saw these complete data backup generations in both the backup catalog and file system:

2026-04-26 02:00
2026-04-27 02:00
2026-04-28 02:00

However, when I checked again on Apr 29, the Apr 26 backup generation had been deleted, and the remaining backup files were:

2026-04-27 02:00
2026-04-28 02:00
2026-04-29 02:00

So the retention policy seems to be working, but not exactly as I initially expected.

My questions are:

  1. Is this behavior expected for Retain Backup Generations Younger Than = 1 day?
  2. Does HANA Cockpit retention evaluate and delete old backup generations only once per daily scheduler run, instead of deleting them immediately when they become older than 1 day?
  3. Why would the Apr 26 generation still exist on Apr 28 but be deleted on Apr 29?
  4. Is the correct interpretation that HANA deletes complete backup generations based on the scheduler run and generation eligibility, not simply based on the file date?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: SAP Help needed on below items
entry.content: We helped you to accept the answer, since there were no updates after 7 days. You can unaccept it anytime if you have further questions.

entry.title: Re: Budget Design Process for Salary Increases: a functional overview
entry.content: I would like to know how to configure the Current Salary to be calculated and displayed as a prorated amount. Currently, the Current Salary in the Budget Assignment page shows the employee’s full-year salary. However, for employees who join mid-year or do not start on January 1st, the salary should be prorated based on their length of service. How can this be configured so that the Compensation Admin can allocate the budget accurately for each team head?

if there any step to make default billing settings to all user, please let know how to maintain it.

 

thankyou

entry.title: Sending billing documents to multiple email addresses if customer and payer are different
entry.content:
Hi all,

We have some invoices where the payer is different to the customer. Currently, these are emailed to the payer, but we would like to also CC the customer's email address.

I understand it is possible to set up additional email recipients through Output Parameter Determination, but I haven't been able to find any information about how to do this specific thing. Is anyone able to please provide any advice?

We are using S4Hana Cloud Public.

Thanks

entry.title: Request new S-user but no enrollment email sent to the user
entry.content:
Requested new S-user.

This user is part of my team. I had raised a request for him to have a login. But he did not get the enrollment mail, I subsequently deleted his entry so that I could recreate it new. His user is still lying in pending for creation.

Could you please expedite the process?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Request new S-user but no enrollment email sent to th...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

If new S-user has been created successfully but user didn't get the welcome email to activate the new S-user, then could you pls kindly refer to the following KBA:

  • KBA 2490845 - New S-user ID did not receive a Welcome e-mail to set their password - SAP for Me

and access the URL https://accounts.sap.com/ui/createForgottenPasswordMail?spId=55365985e4b07dc3abdfc16c&targetUrl=&sourceUrl= to set the new password with the 'Forgot my password' feature?

Hope this works.

Thanks and regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Basic and extended document splitting in SAP S/4 Hana
entry.content:
We have been trying to add the new custom fields for GL document splitting, but the fields are not available to add in the config. node - 'Define Document Splitting Characteristics for General Ledger Accounting'.

The custom fields were created through CFL and are already available in ACDOCA, but for some reason not available to add in the config. node 'Define Document Splitting Characteristics for General Ledger Accounting'.

Can you pls suggest if ACDOCA custom fields can be added as Doc Splitting characteristics.

entry.content:

The custom fields were created through CFL and are already available in ACDOCA, but for some reason not available to add in the config. node 'Define Document Splitting Characteristics for General Ledger Accounting'.

Can you pls suggest if ACDOCA custom fields can be added as Doc Splitting characteristics.

entry.title: Question Regarding Authorization Requirement for Finance Plan Import (BUKRS_GLRLDNR_CATEGORY_P)
entry.content:
Hello Team,

We encountered the following authorization error when the customer attempted to import finance plan data:

Error Message: FINS_ACDOCP_PLANNING056
You do not have authorization to save planning data for source xx, category Z_FI_TAG00, controlling area Axxx, company code 00xx.
Please maintain authorization object K_ACDOCP_P.

As a workaround, we resolved the issue by changing the restriction of the customer's business role "BR_CORP_ADV" for:

Company Code / Ledger / Category (BUKRS_GLRLDNR_CATEGORY_P)
→ Set the entire restriction to Unrestricted (*) for all values

After setting the restriction type to unrestricted for all Company Code / Ledger / Category values, the finance plan import worked successfully.

Question

Does anyone know since when this unrestricted setting for all values of BUKRS_GLRLDNR_CATEGORY_P became required in order to import finance plan data?

Specifically:

  • Was this requirement introduced/changed in a particular version upgrade?
  • Or has this been standard SAP behavior for a long time?

We found a similar KBA dated 2025/11/18, so it appears this may not be related to 2602VUP.
However, we would like to understand when/why this authorization setup became necessary.

Any insight would be appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: HANA Cockpit retention not deleting old SYSTEMDB back...
entry.content:
Hi @FPT ,

Thank you for this very well structured question summary.

I think the HANA Cockpit is functioning as expected.
Please note that the "backup generations" in HANA Cockpit differ from the number of data backup files.
Please refer to SAP Note 2852302 - Backup Retention Policy is not Working as Expected, which provides detailed information on how the retention policy works.
You can find the note here: https://me.sap.com/notes/2852302.

Additional remark, if you check the data backups between April 29, 21:00 PM and April 30, 02:00 AM, I think you will likely find only two data backups remaining:
---------------------------------------
2026-04-28 02:00
2026-04-29 02:00
---------------------------------------

Best regards,
Charles

entry.title: Adding custom columns to the capacity planning ALV in sap EPPM.
entry.content:
Hello,

How to add custom columns to the capacity planning ALV in sap EPPM.

I could find some info on adding columns to financial planning ALV in KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/3253242 .

 

Thanks,

Suhas

 

SAP Portfolio and Project Management

entry.title: Re: Soldoc- Cloud ALM Migration
entry.content:
Hello..

You can run the readiness check report for CALM, it will give you the size of SolDoc content in each branch, based on that data and your current/expected consumption of CALM memory, you can take decision to got for BTP DMS (the only option available now for external file storage) or upload the documents to CALM itself.

Thanks,

Piyush

entry.title: Change Management - Steps to help drive Adoption and Value Realisation from SuccessFactors HCM
entry.content:
Overview

Creating a successful change management strategy requires addressing key business considerations and decisions that align all stakeholders with a unified vision. By collaborating on this process, you can develop your initial program documentation while identifying any gaps and areas needing further exploration.

 

Join us for an interactive webinar where Transformation Advisor Nicole Wright will share real-world examples and leading practices from various industries. This is a unique opportunity to engage with peers, ask questions, and learn from the experiences of professionals across different organizations

 

Who Should Attend

HR and IT Business Leads

Project Sponsors

Change Manager

Project Manager

Super Administrators

HR and IT Functional Leads

SME

 

We will talk about

Program Design

Persona Development 

Communication 

Training Planning 

Sustainment & Reinforcement

SAP Change Management resources

entry.title: Hablemos: Transformando RH con IA
entry.content:
Únete a nuestra sesión de Transformando RH con IA y descubre cómo la inteligencia artificial está revolucionando Recursos Humanos. Exploraremos casos prácticos, herramientas innovadoras y recomendaciones para automatizar procesos, mejorar la experiencia de los empleados y tomar decisiones estratégicas.

¡No te lo pierdas!

entry.title: Hablemos: Gestión de Partners
entry.content:
Únete a nuestra sesión de Gestión de Partners y descubre cómo fortalecer y optimizar la colaboración con proveedores y aliados estratégicos. Conoceremos las capacidades clave de la solución para gestionar de forma estructurada los procesos de interacción con partners, así como mejores prácticas para incrementar la eficiencia operativa, garantizar la seguridad de la información y mejorar la experiencia de colaboración.

¡Te esperamos!

entry.title: Efficient Data Management - Data Governance and Data Catalogue
entry.content:
Steps to establish robust data governance to maximize the value of data as a strategic asset, driving better decision-making and leveraging big data for organizational growth and success

 

Overview

Managing a data governance framework can feel like taming an unruly beast. Multiple data sources, siloed teams, and unclear governance lead to confusion and risks. Take back control with proven Data Governance Transformation Programs. Introducing 1:Few Sessions for SAP SuccessFactors customers.

In this interactive webinar,  Data Governance SAP expert and Transformation Advisor Nicole Wright will share real-world examples and leading practices, providing an opportunity for customers to collaborate, share insights, and learn from colleagues in other organizations.

 

 

Why should you attend?

 

In these interactive sessions, SAP SuccessFactors customers will network and gain insights to tame their data governance frameworks. Attendees will also have the opportunity to exchange ideas and experiences in an open peer-to-peer format, strengthening their ability to manage data as a strategic asset and ensure compliance, security, and accuracy across their HR tech ecosystems.

 

We will talk about:

Relevant programs to be successful at your transformation

•Data Management Strategy

•Data Governance

•Data Governance Charter

•Operating Framework and Responsibilities

•Plan for Operational Success and HR Data Catalogue

•HR Data Governance

•Data Governance Roadmap

•Data Governance and AI

Data Catalogue

entry.title: Let's Talk: Value Management
entry.content: Prepare to engage in an interactive discussion between SuccessFactors customers and SAP experts, where we will explore ideas to build a Value Dashboard with clear goals and KPIs to monitor performance and show results on your cloud enabled transformation

Good Morning All

 

Thank you for the registration to the Data Governance event

Kindly use the following details to register and dial into the event

Efficient Data Management - Data Governance and Data Catalogue | Meeting-Join | Microsoft Teams

 

Warm regards

Nicole

 

 

Steps to establish robust data governance to maximize the value of data as a strategic asset, driving better decision-making and leveraging big data for organizational growth and success

 

Overview

Managing a data governance framework can feel like taming an unruly beast. Multiple data sources, siloed teams, and unclear governance lead to confusion and risks. Take back control with proven Data Governance Transformation Programs. Introducing 1:Few Sessions for SAP SuccessFactors customers.

In this interactive webinar,  Data Governance SAP expert and Transformation Advisor Nicole Wright will share real-world examples and leading practices, providing an opportunity for customers to collaborate, share insights, and learn from colleagues in other organizations.

 

 

Why should you attend?

 

In these interactive sessions, SAP SuccessFactors customers will network and gain insights to tame their data governance frameworks. Attendees will also have the opportunity to exchange ideas and experiences in an open peer-to-peer format, strengthening their ability to manage data as a strategic asset and ensure compliance, security, and accuracy across their HR tech ecosystems.

 

We will talk about:

Relevant programs to be successful at your transformation

•Data Management Strategy

•Data Governance

•Data Governance Charter

•Operating Framework and Responsibilities

•Plan for Operational Success and HR Data Catalogue

•HR Data Governance

•Data Governance Roadmap

•Data Governance and AI

Data Catalogue

entry.title: Steps to shape the ideal sustainment model to get the best results from SuccessFactors HXM
entry.content:
Good Morning

 

 

 

Managing an HR tech ecosystem can feel like wrestling an unruly beast. Multiple modules, siloed teams, and unclear governance lead to headaches. Take back control with proven Transformation Programs. Introducing 1:Few Sessions for SAP SuccessFactors customers.

 

In this interactive webinar, Transformation Advisor Nicole Wright, together with SAP SuccessFactors  Release Management Expert and SAP HR guest speaker will share real-world examples and leading practices  to different customers where you can share, ask and learn from your colleagues in other organizations

 

Who Should Attend

HR and IT Business Leads

Project Sponsors

Change Manager

Project Manager

Super Administrators

HR and IT Functional Leads

SME

 

We will talk about

Relevant programs to be successful at your transformation
• Agile governance models to streamline cloud solutions

• Release Management
• Clear roles and responsibilities for HR, IT, and other stakeholders
• Setting up Service Delivery Model to support the sustainment of SuccessFactors

• Setting up Operating Model to support the sustainment of SuccessFactors
• SF Admin Capabilities


 

We look forward to having you attend the event!

entry.title: Add value to Sales Order header Usage dropdown; config path checked but not found.
entry.content: We need to add a new value to the Usage dropdown in the Sales Order header. We checked the configuration path but couldn’t find where this field is maintained. Please help identify the correct configuration or table to update this dropdown.

entry.title: Re: Unmanaged RAP — Deep Insert, CUD Operations & Action Handling
entry.content:
Hey @NishaBais , 
You haven't provided the method or code to update for child entity.
As there is only just one status update condition in action that you have written. 

I need to know this,
If I update the child entity, on object page does the code requires to put in create method of parent implementation or child implementation class?

entry.title: My transport to BW UAT is failing even after trying all the solutions.
entry.content:
I added a field to 0EQUIPMENT and marked it as a Navigational attribute. This 0EQUIPMENT is directly used in composite provider so the flag Usable as Info provider is checked, and I haven't made any changes in the checkboxes. 

Everything was OK in DEV but while importing it in QB1 (UAT), I am getting the error: Characteristic 0EQUIPMENT is disabled as an InfoProvider, but HCPR exists

Message No. R7B351

Diagnosis

Characteristic 0EQUIPMENT is used in other objects (in a SAP HANA CompositeProvider for example). Characteristic 0EQUIPMENT must therefore be flagged as an InfoProvider.

 

Procedure

If you want to disable the InfoProvider setting, delete characteristic 0EQUIPMENT from the objects where it is used.

. I have tried all the solutions, but nothing is working. It is needed the flag to be checked.

entry.title: Re: SAP ByDesign ManageEmailActivityIn
entry.content:
Hello Lewis,

I am not a PDI expert, but I'd like to share something about the standard function on sales order output in ByD system. You may check if the standard function fill your request. 

In sales orders, you  can manually click 'submit - send order confirmation to account' button. System then generates the output document (sales order confirmation) and sends it to the account. In case mail is set as the output channel, a mail with the sales order confirmation PDF will be sent out. 

In case, you are using web service to release the sales order, you can set the auto-send when the order is released. see details in the KBA 2914079 (https://me.sap.com/notes/0002914079).

Hope the info is helpful.

Thanks,

Adie

entry.title: Re: Validation Error: Exception Subprocess should start w...
entry.content:
Hi Anil,

Exception subprocess always starts with 'Error Start' and ends with 'End Message'. 

Regards,
Sachin

entry.title: Error I::000 Enter AC parts while creating New Product in SAP S/4HANA FIORI Product Master Data
entry.content: This is urgent! Unnecessary Errors are popping up in the practice server. Help!!

entry.title: ALM Health Monitoring With Number Ranges Warning
entry.content:
ALM  Health Monitoring With Number Ranges Warning, 100%

We have checked (Atatched ) the current ranges and they are will below the usages

entry.title: AI in Healthcare: Inviting Ideas, Challenges, and Perspectives
entry.content:
Reimagining Healthcare with AI: A Call for Collaboration

Healthcare is going through a significant shift today. With increasing data availability and rapid advancements in Artificial Intelligence (AI), there’s a growing opportunity to rethink how healthcare systems operate - from day-to-day processes to long-term decision-making.

As part of the CIS Innovation Incubation team, we’ve been exploring how AI can be applied in practical ways to improve efficiency, support better decisions, and create meaningful value for healthcare providers and stakeholders.

Why Healthcare?

Healthcare plays a critical role in society, covering areas like patient care, operations, supply chain, compliance, and planning. At the same time, many of these processes still depend heavily on manual effort, disconnected systems, and delayed insights.

There’s already a large amount of data available across healthcare systems, but it is often underutilised. This creates an opportunity to use AI more effectively.

Some areas where AI could help include:

  • Making sense of large and complex datasets
  • Reducing repetitive and manual tasks
  • Supporting faster and more accurate decision-making
  • Enabling more proactive and predictive operations

What We’re Trying to Do

Our goal is to better understand real-world challenges and explore where AI can genuinely add value.

We are focusing on:

  • Identifying practical pain points in healthcare processes
  • Understanding challenges faced by customers and partners
  • Exploring ideas collaboratively instead of building in isolation

Where AI Could Potentially Help

Without going into specific solutions, some broad areas where AI seems promising include:

  • Analysing data to generate quicker and more useful insights
  • Improving planning and resource utilisation
  • Automating document-heavy or repetitive workflows
  • Making information easier to access through conversational tools
  • Monitoring processes in real time to identify risks early

How We’re Approaching This

We’re taking a collaborative and iterative approach:

  • Engaging with Product Managers and healthcare teams
  • Having discussions to understand real challenges
  • Exploring ideas through quick prototypes where possible

The focus is on staying practical and grounded in real needs.

Call for Inputs

If you’re working in healthcare, your perspective would be really valuable:

  • What are the key challenges you face today?
  • Which processes are the most time-consuming or error-prone?
  • Where do you see real potential for AI adoption?

Your inputs will help us focus on solving the right problems.

What’s Next

Based on the feedback we receive, we’ll:

  • Identify high-impact areas
  • Build initial prototypes
  • Iterate with stakeholders

Conclusion

AI has strong potential to improve how healthcare systems operate, but the real value lies in solving meaningful problems. Collaboration will be key to making that happen.

Looking forward to hearing your thoughts.

entry.title: Re: Introducing Application Vulnerability Report for Cloudfoundry Applications – Try It Now!
entry.content:

just noticed that with the latest update the Application Vulnerability Report has now also it's own UI. But unfortunately this causes an issue in the SAP Build Work Zone Content Expolorer. I've filed SAP Support Case 564722/2026. Hope you can directly pick it up and provide a solution. It's currently blocking us from adding new content to the Launchpad.

Best regards,
Gregor

entry.title: Re: Req Object is not getting passed to bound action
entry.content:
Thank you for your answer!
Sadly, using an explicit arrow function does not solve the problem. super.init() is getting called in the init().
Event bubbling could be a problem.

In general, the structure of my schema.cds should be pretty common. There should be at least a known issue described by SAP or somone had the same problem and posted about it, but there isn't. Maybe there is something else that we both missing?

entry.title: Re: Add New Fields in My Inbox for SAP Flexible Workflow
entry.content:
Hi, What you are looking for can be achieved Via Custom attributes.

In the following note you can find information about:

2501626 - Show more Attributes in Fiori My Inbox Application

Hope this helps!

entry.title: Norway VAT ID (NO1) not printed on invoice despite correct tax determination
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

we are currently facing an issue with VAT ID printing for Norway and would appreciate your support.

Problem description:
A Norwegian VAT ID (format: NO1) is maintained in the customer master data, but it is not printed on the invoice, although everything seems to be correctly configured.

Details:

  • Ship-to country/region: Norway (NO)
  • Tax departure country/region: Germany (DE)
  • Tax code: A7
  • Tax determination: Working correctly

We also observed that this issue can occur when the country of the VAT ID does not match the customer's country. However, in this case, there is no mismatch, so this should not be the root cause.

Question:
What could be the reason that the Norwegian VAT ID is not printed on the invoice despite correct master data and tax determination? Are there specific configuration settings, forms, or routines that control this behavior for Norway?

Any hints or guidance would be highly appreciated.

Thank you in advance!

Kathi

entry.title: Re: SAP on Azure: Backup SAP HANA databases with HSR enabled on Azure VMs
entry.content:
Thanks for this document.

We have implemented this succesfully and backups are running fine, however, restore from HANA Studio does not seem possible, it throws error:

From official doc link the restore options listed are Azure portal and Azure CLI.

Does that mean that we can not restore HSR databases with Azure backup from HANA Studio? Anyone has any experience with this?

Thanks!!!

entry.title: reduce Field Length of System Database Field
entry.content:
Hi All

It is not possible to reduce the Field length of Sales Order Customer PO Reference Field (NumatCard) from 200 to 100 right?

 

Kedalene Chong

entry.title: Re: Use other external SMS/Email providers
entry.content:
Hi,

SAP’s response is correct — out-of-the-box, CDC primarily supports listed SMS/email providers, and anything outside that typically requires using Extensions or custom integration logic.

Just to add from implementation experience:
If you’re planning to use a provider like Odigo (or any non-listed provider), the usual approach is:

  • Use CDC Extensions / Webhooks to trigger an external API call
  • Handle OTP/TFA logic outside CDC (your backend or middleware)
  • Return status back to CDC flow for verification

This gives you full flexibility in choosing regional or specialized providers, especially if you need better delivery performance in specific markets.

One thing to keep in mind — when working with external SMS providers, make sure they support:

  • Reliable DLR (delivery reports)
  • Low-latency routing for OTP use cases
  • Failover/fallback (important for authentication flows)

We’ve seen this approach work well particularly in regions where default providers don’t perform consistently.

If helpful, there are providers that focus specifically on high-delivery routing in emerging markets (Africa, Middle East, etc.), which can be integrated via API in this same extension-based setup.

Hope this helps clarify

entry.title: Re: Inquiry on Value-Based Automatic Free Goods Determina...
entry.content:
Hello @dawid90,

When we are searching for something, we are expecting a solution rather than a generic explanation.

Try to understand; we knew that there is no standard BADI to enhance the value base free goods determination, and in standard it's not possible. 
If you have any solution for it, you have to explain it to me in detail; please don't share any generic explanations. We want results-driven answer. 

I hope you can understand. 
Thanks
Hasnat

entry.title: SAP Settlement Management – Retrieve PDF Form via API for Lobster Integration (XInvoice/ZUGFeRD)
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I'm facing an integration challenge in the area of SAP Settlement Management (Condition Contract Management) and hope someone has had similar experiences or can point me in the right direction.

Background:

My customer uses Lobster as their EDI/middleware solution. For standard billing, the integration is already working: Lobster retrieves the invoice PDF via the standard OData API:

 
 
GET /sap/opu/odata/SAP/API_BILLING_DOCUMENT_SRV/GetPDF

This works smoothly and is well documented.

The Problem:

Now we need to build an analogous process for the Customer Rebate Document from Settlement Management – i.e., the document issued to the customer during bonus settlement (Customer Rebate Settlement Document / credit memo equivalent).

The goal is to also retrieve this form via an interface and pass it to Lobster, so it can be further processed and sent as an XInvoice (XRechnung) or ZUGFeRD-compliant electronic invoice.

My Research So Far:

I have searched extensively but have not found a comparable standard API:

  • On the SAP API Business Hub (api.sap.com), there does not appear to be a dedicated OData API for Settlement Management forms / Condition Contract documents
  • API_BILLING_DOCUMENT_SRV only covers classic SD billing documents, not Settlement Management documents
  • I am not aware of any RFC-enabled function modules that cover this use case

My Questions for the Community:

  1. Is there a (perhaps lesser-known) OData or REST API in the SAP standard that allows programmatic retrieval of a Settlement Management form PDF?
  2. Would an RFC/BAPI approach be feasible?
  3. Are there alternative integration approaches, such as:
    • Direct integration via SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) / Integration Suite?
    • Output via SPAD/Spool with subsequent retrieval via web service?
    • Using Smart Forms / Adobe Forms Output Management with a downstream retrieval step?
  4. Has anyone successfully implemented this process – rebate settlement document → middleware → XInvoice/ZUGFeRD delivery – and can share their approach?

System Environment: SAP S/4HANA (On-Premise), Lobster as middleware, target format XRechnung / ZUGFeRD 2.x

Any hint is appreciated – even a confirmed "this is not possible in standard" would already be helpful and move us forward. Thanks in advance!

Best regards

entry.title: Electronic Bank Statement Process in SAP S4 HANA
entry.content:
Introduction

This blogs aims to explore the importance of Bank Reconciliation process within the SAP S4 HANA system. I have tried to cover the key features, functionalities, implementation considerations and challenges that have experience in my implementation journey.

Importance of Bank Reconciliation

Bank statements are very critical for any firm as they give them a clear understanding of their receivables and payables. This also helps them to understand their accurate cash balances to make any informed decisions.

Bank Reconciliation process helps the firm to understand any discrepancies that might arise due to the few of the following reasons below.

  1. Timing of the transactions
  2. Unknown transactions
  3. Payments received but not cleared

Understanding Electronic Bank Statement Reconciliation Process

EBS Process

 External bank statements are imported into S/4HANA (e.g. CAMT.053) and processed via batch job (FF_5 or Import Bank Statement App).

Once the statement is imported in the system, it attempts automatic matching bank statement items to accounting postings i.e. open items on the bank G/L, customer/vendor clears, payments received/paid. Unmatched items are reviewed and handled in the Post‑Processing Worklist (manual reconciliation, additional postings for bank charges, interest, FX differences or adjustments).

 Matched items are cleared in the bank G/L (reconciliation) account; after reconciliation and any necessary adjustments, the balance of the Main bank G/L will equal the reconciled bank statement balance.

Key Features of Bank Reconciliation in S4 HANA

  • Automated bank‑statement processing: S/4HANA imports statements in common formats (MT940, BAI2, CAMT.053, etc.) and posts them automatically, cutting down manual data entry and intervention.
  • Real‑time posting and reporting: Reconciliations and postings update immediately, giving an accurate, up‑to‑date view of cash positions.
  • Exception handling and manual clearing: Items that cannot be matched automatically are flagged for review and cleared or posted manually.
  • Audit trail and compliance: All reconciliation actions are logged, providing a clear audit trail and supporting regulatory compliance.
  • Improved user experience: SAP Fiorio apps gives enhance features to process and clear the bank statement items (Reprocess bank statement item app, Manage reprocessing rules etc)

Electronic Bank Statement Process Flow

 

 

 

 Key Steps in Bank Reconciliation 

  • Verify Completeness of Imported Bank Statements: Verify that the imported Electronic Bank Statements are error-free and complete.
  • Post bank statements are imported into SAP S4 system: With Electronic Bank Statements (EBS) enabled and imported daily, Bank Reconciliation Analysts can generate reports to compare bank and book balances as often as needed. To facilitate this process, the following Fiori applications can be used to compare book-bank balances.

    Reprocess Bank Statement items: This app provides comprehensive details of bank statement items, along with their posting and processing status. It enables business users to Identify transactions that have not been fully posted yet (Colored in Yellow and Red).

    Additionally, the user can filter the data based on different parameters

    1. Notes to Payee information
    2. BTC Code (Identifies the nature of transaction).

     

  • Clearing of Cash and Cash Equivalent G/L account Open items

  • There are several open item managed G/L accounts that are established as clearing accounts to facilitate the automatic posting of bank statement items through Electronic Bank Statements.

    Typically, clearing accounts are processed automatically when bank statements are imported. However, due to insufficient data to automate the process, some clearing accounts must be cleared manually.

    Below are the 3 steps in which clearing takes place:

    1. Clearing with EBS: Standard EBS functionality offers automatic clearing of clearing accounts, if it meets the specified clearing criteria.
    2. Automatic Clearing Program (F.13): For certain clearing accounts where the EBS automatic clearing does not apply, the Automatic Clearing Program can be utilized.
    3. Manual Clearing: If the automatic clearing criteria are not met, then the clearing of open items will be handled manually.

    Below are some benefits the organization received by implementing EBS.

     

    • Faster Month-End Close: Automation of bank statement helped to reduce the month-end close as the jobs are executed daily giving more time for the analyst to identify the issues and resolve on a daily basis.
    • Improved Accuracy and Compliance: Automation minimizes manual intervention, ensuring more accurate financial statements. Every processing step is logged, supporting audit and compliance requirements.
    • Real time visibility: Electronic bank statements get processed real-time, giving true update on company’s cash position.
    • Manage Reprocessing Rules: Reconciliation Analyst can create rules based on identical bank statements items that similar and follow the same posting rules using S/4 Fiori app.

     

     

Challenges & Lessons learned from my project:

 

Bank statement does not include unique external transaction (BTC or BAI Code) for Bank-to-Bank Account Transfers and update trading partner.

 Solution: Leverage unique identifier from the Memo Line and configure a search string to post to the desired posting rule. Use search string to update the trading partner using BDC field and value fields.

 Foreign payments – Exchange rate difference was being posted to DSA account rather FX Gain/loss Account.

 Solution: Use substitution rule to post the difference between bank and book balances to FX gain/loss account. Setup tolerance group for user and G/L account.

 Payment Document number as string was sent in bank statement under End to End id tag instead of Payment reference which did not allow to auto-clear outgoing clearing account. 

Solution: Use the search string to extract Payment document number from the string and assign interpretation algorithm 020-Doc number search to auto-clear the outgoing clearing account.

 Conclusion

In SAP S/4HANA, bank reconciliation is enhanced through automation, real-time processing, and seamless integration with other modules. These advancements reduce manual effort, minimize errors, and provide instant visibility into cash positions and exceptions. Ultimately, effective bank reconciliation fosters financial transparency, strengthens internal controls, and accelerates the financial close, making it a strategic enabler for modern finance teams.

entry.title: Does S/4HANA Really Support 10 Currencies? Fact vs. Myth
entry.content:
Applicability: S/4HANA On-Premise and Private Cloud
Read Time: 4 Minutes

SAP S/4HANA Finance FIN General Ledger SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition  SAP S/4HANA 

Recently, I was working with a multinational client who had complex multi-currency requirements. They needed to track:

We often hear the claim that S/4HANA supports up to 10 currencies, a massive jump from the limits of ECC. But as I worked through the implementation, I realized it is vital to distinguish between what is "supported" and what is "fully integrated."

The Myth: "All 10 Currencies Are Created Equal"

While it is a fact that the Universal Journal (ACDOCA) has fields for up to 10 currencies (WSL, TSL, HSL, KSL, and OSL through GSL), it is a myth that they all behave the same way in every process.

The technical reality is that the BSEG table has not been extended. It still only holds a maximum of 3 parallel currencies. These are your 1st, 2nd, and 3rd FI Currencies configured in transaction FINSC_LEDGER.

Why the "BSEG Limit" Still Matters

You might wonder: if we have ACDOCA, why care about the old BSEG table?

The answer lies in legacy processes and historical data. Many programs still rely on BSEG logic. Unless you have Universal Parallel Accounting (UPA) activated (available since S/4HANA 2022), currencies beyond the first three—those not integrated with BSEG—face certain limitations:

  • Historical Rate Gaps: Processes like Fixed Asset Accounting (specifically Asset Under Construction settlements) and Material Ledger often require historical exchange rates.
  • The Fallback Effect: For the 4th currency and beyond (that is the non-integrated BSEG currencies), the system may not have the historical data stored. In these cases, it falls back to the current exchange rate.
  • Rounding Differences: This fallback can lead to differences caused by rounding or varying exchange rates that wouldn't exist in the BSEG-integrated currencies.

The Strategy: Choosing Your "BSEG Relevant" Currencies

Because only 3 currencies can be integrated with BSEG, you must choose them wisely. A key rule to remember is that currency types in the customer namespace (Z) cannot be BSEG-relevant*. You must use standard SAP currency types.

One possible approach  was:
To ensure maximum integration and accuracy for their IFRS and Group needs, we carefully mapped their three BSEG slots:

  1. 1st Currency: Company Code Currency (10)
  2. 2nd Currency: Group/Global Currency (30)
  3. 3rd Currency: Hard Currency (40)

We chose Hard Currency (40) to meet the EUR reporting requirement. While Hard Currency is typically intended for high-inflation regions, it was the most appropriate standard currency type, in our scenario, to ensure BSEG integration and avoid the "fallback" issues associated with freely defined currencies.

!! Note:
In this particular scenario, we selected Currency Type 40 (Hard Currency) for our third BSEG slot to meet EUR reporting needs. However, this should not be taken as a universal rule. Every client environment is different.

When Should You Use the Other 7 Currencies?

In my opinion, if your goal is primarily Reporting, the 4th through 10th currencies are excellent. They are supported in:

  • Fiori Apps (like Journal Entry Analyzer and Audit Journal)
  • G/L Account Line-Item reports (FAGLL03, FAGLL03H)
  • CDS-based Analytics

Conclusion: S/4HANA does indeed provide 10 currency fields, but the "10-currency" claim is only a partial truth for those not using UPA. For deep financial processes, the "Power of 3" still rules.

Important Disclaimer: The configuration choices mentioned above were specific to a particular business case. A thorough impact analysis of your unique business requirements are required before making any currency configuration suggestions.

entry.title: Re: Problem: How to make a custom field non-editable when editing a sales order
entry.content:
Hi Slanzetta,

if you added your custom field via the Custom Fields or Custom Fields and Logic app, I would first suggest checking the configuration there.

Open the app, search for your field, and navigate to the User Interface tab. Then select the relevant UI (via the arrow on the right). In some cases, SAP already provides out-of-the-box functionality to mark the field as read-only for a specific UI.

If this option is not available, you can alternatively implement a BDEF extension for the corresponding (extended) CDS projection view entity. For example:

extension for projection;
extend behavior for C_YOURCONSUMEVIEWENTITY
{
  field ( readonly )
    ZZ1_SettCont_SDH;
}

Best regards

entry.title: Re: SAP Make-to-Order Policy: When entering the manufactu...
entry.content:
Good day @shiwa 

Thank you for your question.

The scenario you described is not only a production version issue. How the Public Cloud handles make-to-order planning and individual customer segments matters here. See KBA 3485915 on MTO and ETO scenarios and its area of performance. In a nutshell, it is not possible to plan individual customer segments in a separate MRP area. Consult Note 550844 for further details.

So, in your case, the behaviour is expected if your process depends on storage location level planning or MRP area logic for a make-to-order requirement. What this basically means is that:

  • Strategy 20 creates individual customer requirements.
  • These individual customer requirements are planned in the plant MRP area.
  • The storage location you entered in the sales order item does not make the individual customer segment planned in a storage location MRP area.
  • Storage location MRP logic is not the right design for MTO individual segments.
  • You should not use the production version’s goods receipt storage location as the mechanism to drive component issue storage locations for individual customer segments.
  • If no production version is selected, make sure you check whether the selection criteria and master data are still valid at plant level, not at the areal level of the storage location MRP.

See the following reference for component storage location requirement and production version.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2bba750d1e124e1ea2a039bb1cd9b6c5/59acce5314894208e10000000a174cb4.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2bba750d1e124e1ea2a039bb1cd9b6c5/c234b8535c39b44ce10000000a174cb4.html?locale=en-US

So, to answer your specific requirement, if the what you need is that component issue storage location should be proposed automatically based on individual customer segment requirements, then there is no standard way to achieve this by using storage location MRP areas in an MTO strategy 20 scenario.

I recommend you separate the two topics:

  • Option 1 is to use plant level MTO planning and standard component storage location determination. Keep the MTO planning at plant level. Then use the supported BOM explosion logic to maintain component storage location determination.
  • Option 2 is to use the production version to determine the BOM and routing, and the receipt storage location for the produced material. Do not use it as the main driver for component issue storage locations.

If your customer requirement strictly depends on storage location level MTO planning, the process design should be reviewed. The closest standard approach is to use plant level MTO planning and determine the component issue storage location through the supported options.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Partner Solutions for Visual Inspections with SAP Digital Manufacturing
entry.content:
Dear @tobias_weiblen ,
I recommend to contact your responsible SAP Account Manager to discuss this topic. Thank you.
Best regards
Manfred

entry.title: Re: New here!
entry.content: You are Welcome

entry.title: Re: Can reporting FS item REPT be used in the standard FS...
entry.content:
Hi,

You cannot mix FS Item and Reporting items in one hierarchy.
- You can add reporting items to a FS Item hierarchy but it won't give you any values.

If I was to create this I would look into a custom query with a structure or see if the review booklet could help.

I don't think to create one-to-one relationship between INC, EXP etc. will be a very pretty way forward.

Hope this helps you forward.

Br
Martin Jul

entry.title: Change Company address
entry.content:
We moved to a new Address.

Please change the address to our company (customer number), thank you.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Change Company address
entry.content:
Dear customer,

If you need to change the company address of your customer number, could you pls kindly refer to KBA 2602869 and contact your SAP Account Executive?

If you do not know who your SAP Account Executive is, refer to KBA 2626344. 

Then your account manager will work with the SAP Order Management team to change the company address for you.

Thanks and best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Join the Webinar: Implementing Polish SAF-T with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance
entry.content: Hi Rachid, thanks a lot for your comment. If you are interested in KSeF 2.0 with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, I can recommend the webinar replay: Electronic Invoices in Poland (KSeF 2.0) with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance. We also offer an integration video: Integrating and Setting Up the Cloud Edition for Electronic Invoices in Poland Using KSeF 2.0. For regular updates join the Regional Collaboration Group SAP Document and Reporting Compliance for Poland open for customers and partners. Best regards, Janina.

entry.title: Re: Deactivate the use of Reference Exchange Rate Indicat...
entry.content:
Hi,

I can just add.

The effect from the income statement will always be 0 from the currency translation.

Your net income/loss is reversed on account 799000. This account is treated as rest of the income statement. No matter the difference between CLO and AVG, FS Item 799000 will offset the rest of PL.

Br

Martin Jul

entry.title: 1H2026 Release: A journey to Autonomous Payroll with SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll
entry.content:
Hello Everyone!

To advance our vision of an autonomous payroll, we are introducing four specialized Payroll Agents for the SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll (ECP) solution in this first half 2026 release.

You can now benefit from automations across 1) payroll and time explanations, 2) alert resolution, 3) rule creation, and 4) data integration. These AI agents detect, explain, and resolve issues before they impact your workforce. They streamline how payroll teams prepare and run payroll by orchestrating checks across time, workflow, and payroll controls.

Below are the key highlights, but be sure to explore the What's New Viewer for the complete release notes and full details on everything we’re delivering.

Payroll Explanation Agent

With this update, the Payroll Explanation Agent connects Payroll and Time sheet data into one unified explanation, giving employees a clear, easy‑to‑understand view of their pay statements and the time data behind them. Employees get crisp, clear and fast answers without relying on HR. This enhances employee experience, builds trust and confidence in payroll accuracy and lowers operational costs. PostNL, one of our customers based in Netherlands, expects 80% cost reduction in handling payroll questions with an AI agent. Additionally, support now extends to Brazil and Canada, building on the 2025 release in the USA, UK, Australia, Mexico, the Netherlands, and Germany.

  • See the Payroll Explanation Agent in action here (video)

 

[Screenshot] Payroll Explanation Agent now integrates Time sheet data and supports Brazil and Canada

Payroll Alert Resolution Agent

This agent streamlines how payroll administrators manage Payroll Control Center (PCC) alerts by turning complex investigations into a guided, intelligent workflow. It explains why alerts occur, identifies the most likely root causes, and recommends prioritized fixes directly within the payroll control center. Administrators can also create new validation rules using natural‑language through a conversational interface.

  • See the Payroll Alert Resolution Agent in action here (video)

[Screenshot] Payroll Alert Resolution Agent in ECP

Payroll Validation Rule Agent

This rule creation agent introduces a conversational, AI‑assisted way for payroll administrators to build new validation rules directly within the Payroll Control Center. Instead of navigating technical configuration steps, admins simply describe the rule they need in natural language.

Instead of navigating technical configuration steps, admins can simply describe the rule they need in natural language making the whole process of validation rule creation 50% faster.

[Screenshot] Payroll Validation Rule Agent in ECP

Employee Data Integration Agent

The agent helps payroll administrator resolve payroll replication errors by translating complex technical messages into clear, SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central–focused guidance. It identifies impacted EC fields based on ECP mappings and provides step‑by‑step recommendations for fixing data issues. This release addresses manual corrections.

For example, a missing cost center for an employee. Instead of leaving the admin to interpret a technical error message, the agent analyzes organizational data and suggests the most likely cost center—based on where the employee’s peers in the same department and reporting to the same manager are assigned.

The result: cleaner data, fewer payroll interruptions, and far less time spent resolving issues after the fact.

  • See the Employee Data Integration Agent in action here (video)

[Screenshot] Employee Data Integration Agent in ECP

 

What Else is New this Release?

There are a number of additional innovations available for SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll in the first half 2026 release, including

  • Enhancements to Payroll Control Center: Manage Payroll Activities
  • Enhanced Gender Field Replication from Employee Central to Employee Central Payroll
  • Dozens of legal changes for specific country/region (documented in detail in the Regulatory Change Manager)
  • And more

See the What's New Viewer for more information on these features.

Where Can I Find More Information?

Here are additional resources for those interested in learning more about SAP Payroll solutions

  • SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll Website
  • SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll SAP Help (Implementation) (Requires ID)
  • SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll Community Page (Discussion Forum) (Requires ID)
  • SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll Learning Journey (Educational Content) (Requires SAP Lea...
  • Other recent release highlights for SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll
    • 2H/2025 Release Highlight Blog
    • 1H/2025 Release Highlight Blog
    • 2H/2024 Release Highlight Blog
    • 1H/2024 Release Highlight Blog

For information on release highlights across SAP SuccessFactors HCM, visit this one stop shop for all product release and road map information

entry.title: Best Group for MRO questions?
entry.content:
Hi all. I wanted to pick the brains of this large community which some questions/experiences around rolling out SAP S4HANA for a large organization with multiple business units from an MRO perspective. They currently use an array of processes/systems and the goal is to align them all on SAP. No easy feat! 

Before I get into asking my questions I wanted to ask what the best group it would be to post this in as it's not that obvious to me? Not really much focus on MRO and organizational transformation from what I can see?

entry.title: How can SAP BTP be used to build scalable healthcare applications?
entry.content: How can SAP BTP help in building healthcare apps that can grow easily when more users or data come in?

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Compatibility Pack: What You Need to Know About SAP Add-on for Polish SAF-T
entry.content: Join our webinar on May 26 for a hands-on demo showing how to set up and use SAP Document and Reporting Compliance for Poland, with practical configurations for JPK_KR_PD (Accounting Books) and JPK_VAT. See how the solution works in real scenarios while ensuring a smooth transition from the SAP Add-on for Polish SAF-T: Go to webinar sign-up: Implement Poland SAF T with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance - A Practical Walkthrough.

entry.title: Re: Add New Fields in My Inbox for SAP Flexible Workflow
entry.content:
Hello @dbx87 


No, you cannot directly add new fields to the approval details in My Inbox for standard workflow WS00800251 via configuration.

Show existing fields using UI Adaptation
Extend data via CDS View extension + annotations
Use BAdI  workflow extension to pass additional data

Not possible - Adding completely new custom fields in My Inbox UI without backend/service extension


Extend the OData service (via CDS) + expose fields → then adapt UI


Thanks,
Sravan
